name: Henrique Painhas
bio: I am an experienced Software Engineer and Android Developer, with previous experience as an athlete, and have a passion for innovation and a strong drive for continuous improvement in all of my endeavors. I am currently reading the book Clean Code by Robert C. Martin and I'm looking forward to applying what I have learned so far on my next projects.
skills_bio: Currently, I'm working as a Software Engineer at Toast, Inc., soaking up as much knowledge and practice as I can in an attempt to get better at my role and advance in my career. I'm a member of the Payments Workflow team and I'm responsible for implementing new features, updating existing payment functionalities, and fixing bugs within the Toast point of sale (POS) application.

education.major: B.S. in Computer and Information Sciences

workExperience.name: Toast Inc.
workExperience.description: Member of the Payments Workflow team. Responsible for implementing new features, updating existing payment functionalities, and fixing bugs within the Toast point of sale (POS) application.

workExperience.name: eRetailing Associates
workExperience.description: Member of the e-commerce team which focuses on making the production team as efficient as possible in order to increase order throughput and inventory accuracy. Designed and implemented an Android application for the Zebra MC40 scanners. The FacilityApp mainly monitors all of the order processing workflow, handles inventory accuracy, and allows the production team to track and locate items with ease. The use of the FacilityApp made it easier for eRetailing to partner with Homage by streamlining the data between both companies and allowing the production team to handle the increase in workload, helping increase the company's revenue.

workExperience.name: Vyra
workExperience.description: Let’s face it. Typical coding challenges and white board questions just aren’t cutting it anymore. We need tools that allow companies to assess candidates on skills that matter. Introducing Vyra. My two friends and I attempted building a startup that would allow tech companies to interview candidates through real work scenarios. The platform included a built-in calendar, dashboard, candidate management tools, real time screen sharing, real time note taking, among other features.

technicalProjects.name: Countries App
technicalProjects.description: Basic Android application that covers all the modern Android development patterns in Java. This includes: MVVM architecture, Lifecycle Extensions, Dagger2, RxJava, Retrofit, Glide, and some unit testing.

technicalProjects.name: DevSocializa
technicalProjects.description: An in-depth full-stack social network application for developers. The tech stack includes Node.js, Express, React, Redux, and MongoDB along with ES6+.

technicalProjects.name: Pokemon App
technicalProjects.description: Very simple React Pokemon application using the latest features of React. It utilizes hooks for state and effects instead of classes, and also basic pagination.

technicalProjects.name: Homeowner Buddy
technicalProjects.description: A cross-platform mobile app which interacts directly with the web app. The mobile app allows Habitat Mid-Ohio homeowners to search for a tutorial video on how to solve a particular home issue they might have. My team and I used Xamarin Forms, REST architecture, and C# to implement the app.

intercollegiateAthletics.name: Ohio State University DI Varsity Men's Swimming and Diving Team
accolades: 2nd place at Men’s 100y Backstroke at 2019 Big Ten Men’s Swimming and Diving Championships
Recipient of the 2018 Ohio State University Swimming & Diving Carl Wirthwein Award (Most Improved Swimmer)
Placed 6 th in the 100m Butterfly at 2015 FINA World Junior Swimming Championships

leadership.name: Design, Develop, Deploy Organization (D3)
leadership.description: Collaboratively worked on a team to develop a platform that would change the way technical interviews work in the tech world.
